creator: truckman@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to leftfoot from comment #12)

1) The patch totally removed the ".if defined(DISABLE_MAKE_JOBS)" section, so no there should not have been anything to change.  In any case /usr/ports/Mk/bsd.port.mk should always define MAKE_JOBS_NUMBER.

2) Strange ... I haven't seen that here.  I've never seen that error message anywhere, though I haven't tried this patch on FreeBSD 9.3.  I have tried this patch on 8.4, 10.1, and 11.0-CURRENT.

re: P.S. Your testing is appreciated.  I'm hoping to get he port to build without problems to benefit others in similar situations.

re: P.S.S. In your situation, the only downside of swap < RAM might be problems with crash dumps.  Heavy swap usage with 8 GB of RAM would be a performance killer.
